title: Subordinate Clauses
summary: How to connect sentences using subordinating conjunctions that send the verb to the end of the clause.

sections.heading: Common Subordinating Conjunctions
body: Subordinating conjunctions introduce a dependent clause and force the conjugated verb to move to the very end of that clause. This is different from main clauses, where the verb sits in second position.

The most important subordinating conjunctions are: weil (because), dass (that), obwohl (although), wenn (if / when – present/future), als (when – single past event), während (while), and damit (so that).
The comma always separates the main clause from the subordinate clause, regardless of which comes first.

examples.de: Ich bleibe zu Hause, weil ich krank bin.
examples.en: I am staying at home because I am ill.

examples.de: Er sagt, dass er morgen kommt.
examples.en: He says that he is coming tomorrow.

examples.de: Sie geht spazieren, obwohl es regnet.
examples.en: She goes for a walk although it is raining.

examples.de: Wenn ich Zeit habe, lese ich ein Buch.
examples.en: When/If I have time, I read a book.

examples.de: Als ich jung war, wohnte ich in Berlin.
examples.en: When I was young, I lived in Berlin.

examples.de: Ich lerne Deutsch, damit ich in Deutschland arbeiten kann.
examples.en: I am learning German so that I can work in Germany.

sections.heading: Word Order in Subordinate Clauses
body: The golden rule: conjunction + subject + (objects / adverbs) + verb (final position).
If the subordinate clause comes first, the main clause begins with the conjugated verb immediately after the comma — the two finite verbs end up next to each other across the comma.
With a modal verb (kann, muss, darf…) the modal goes last and the infinitive sits just before it: '…weil er heute nicht kommen kann.'
With the Perfekt tense, the auxiliary verb (hat/ist) goes to the very end, after the Partizip II: '…weil er das Buch gelesen hat.'

examples.de: Ich lerne viel, weil ich die Prüfung bestehen möchte.
examples.en: I study a lot because I want to pass the exam.

examples.de: Weil es kalt ist, trage ich einen Mantel.
examples.en: Because it is cold, I am wearing a coat.

examples.de: Sie freut sich, dass du gekommen bist.
examples.en: She is happy that you have come.

examples.de: Er geht nicht aus, während es schneit.
examples.en: He does not go out while it is snowing.

examples.de: Ich weiß nicht, ob er Zeit hat.
examples.en: I don't know whether he has time.

sections.heading: Subordinate Clauses in First Position
body: A subordinate clause may lead the sentence. In that case the entire subordinate clause counts as the first element, so the main-clause verb comes immediately after the comma (V2 rule still applies to the main clause).
This structure is common when the speaker wants to emphasise the reason, condition, or concession rather than the main event.

examples.de: Weil ich müde bin, gehe ich früh schlafen.
examples.en: Because I am tired, I am going to bed early.

examples.de: Obwohl es spät ist, lernt sie noch.
examples.en: Although it is late, she is still studying.

examples.de: Als er ankam, war das Essen fertig.
examples.en: When he arrived, the food was ready.

exercises.prompt: Ich esse nicht, weil ich keinen Hunger ___. (haben)
exercises.explanation: In a weil-clause the conjugated verb moves to the end: '…weil ich keinen Hunger habe.'

exercises.question: Which sentence has the correct word order?
options: Er bleibt zu Hause, weil er ist krank.
Er bleibt zu Hause, weil krank er ist.
Er bleibt zu Hause, weil er krank ist.
Er bleibt, weil er krank zu Hause ist.
exercises.explanation: The verb 'ist' must come at the very end of the subordinate clause introduced by 'weil'.
Main clause: 'Ich weiß' (V2). Subordinate clause after 'dass': subject 'sie', adverb 'morgen', verb 'kommt' at the end.

exercises.prompt: Obwohl es regnete, ___ wir spazieren gegangen. (sein)
exercises.explanation: The obwohl-clause comes first; the main clause therefore begins with the finite verb 'sind', followed by the subject 'wir'.

exercises.question: Choose the correct conjunction: 'Sie arbeitet viel, ___ sie viel Geld verdienen möchte.'
exercises.explanation: 'Damit' expresses purpose (so that). She works a lot so that she can earn a lot of money.
